sentence_reviews.text: A policy is adjusted toward preferred behavior while controlling deviation from a reference model.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Ouyang et al. RLHF paper, PPO optimization of a policy against a reward model with KL control to a reference; Rafailov et al. DPO paper, direct preference optimization with implicit reward and reference-policy term.
sentence_reviews.notes: Both cited sources support optimizing toward preferences while constraining deviation from a reference policy/model.

sentence_reviews.text: A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Preference learning, Reward modeling.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families) is modeled as depending on Preference learning, Reward modeling and feeding downstream nodes such as Direct and online preference optimization, Safety policies, constitutional constraints & alignment, Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO).
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.
